findings.evidence: `BernsteinTheorem.lean:7-8` imports both `LaplaceRepresentation` and `BernsteinMeasures`; `BernsteinMeasures.lean:10` already has `public import TauCeti.Analysis.CompletelyMonotone.LaplaceRepresentation`.
findings.fix: Remove the direct `public import TauCeti.Analysis.CompletelyMonotone.LaplaceRepresentation` from `BernsteinTheorem.lean`, or make the dependency non-transitive by changing the lower import structure if that is intended.
findings.issue: `BernsteinTheorem` publicly imports `LaplaceRepresentation` immediately before publicly importing `BernsteinMeasures`, which already publicly imports `LaplaceRepresentation`; this makes line 7 an unused/redundant public import.

summary: The new material is in the right part of the CompletelyMonotone hierarchy, but `BernsteinTheorem` has a redundant public import that is already re-exported by its next import.
